payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Pocket money task as compensated work unit
Task assignment as parent-defined compensated work allocation
Child as primary user claiming and tracking completed pocket money tasks
Child as information consumer viewing task inventory and completion status
Weekly task claiming and tracking cycle
Weekly earnings calculation and reporting cycle
Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks on a weekly basis
Provide children visibility into available and completed task inventory
Child claims completed task for earnings
Weekly earned amount aggregation and presentation to child
Parental aggregated reporting of child earnings and task completion

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming process for completed work units
payload.justification: Signal SG049 describes claiming as a strategic process enabling children to assert task completion.

payload.description: Task tracking process for completion status monitoring
payload.justification: Signal SG049 identifies tracking as a distinct strategic process for monitoring task completion.

payload.description: Task assignment definition process with monetary value configuration
payload.justification: Signal SG050 describes task definition and compensation configuration as a strategic process performed by parents.

payload.description: Compensation distribution process based on child performance reporting
payload.justification: Signal SG053 identifies compensation distribution as a strategic process dependent on performance reporting.

payload.description: Task assignment as parent-defined compensated work unit
payload.justification: Signals SG049, SG050, and SG051 collectively describe tasks as strategic entities central to the enterprise value proposition. | Signal SG050 describes task assignments as distinct strategic entities with monetary values configured by parents.

payload.description: Weekly earnings summary as child compensation aggregation
payload.justification: Signal SG052 identifies weekly earnings summary as a strategic entity representing aggregated child compensation.

payload.description: Child performance report as parent decision-support artifact
payload.justification: Signal SG053 describes performance reporting as a strategic entity supporting parental compensation distribution decisions.

payload.description: Longitudinal task history as accountability and trend data repository
payload.justification: Signal SG054 identifies historical task data as a strategic entity supporting accountability and analytical purposes.

payload.description: Weekly cycle for task claiming, tracking, earnings calculation, and reporting
payload.justification: Signal SG049 explicitly references a weekly cycle governing task claiming and tracking activities. | Signal SG052 references weekly earnings summary, implying a strategic weekly compensation cycle.

payload.description: Child as primary user viewing task inventory, completion status, and claiming completed pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Signal SG049 explicitly identifies children as actors performing task claiming and tracking activities in the enterprise context. | Signal SG051 identifies children as actors requiring visibility into task inventory, distinct from the claiming role.

payload.description: Parent as task administrator defining compensated task assignments
payload.justification: Signal SG050 clearly articulates parents as actors responsible for defining task assignments and monetary values.

payload.description: Child as earnings beneficiary viewing weekly compensation summary
payload.justification: Signal SG052 positions children as actors motivated to view their earnings outcomes.

payload.description: Parent as performance reviewer accessing child reporting for compensation distribution
payload.justification: Signal SG053 establishes parents as actors requiring performance reporting access for compensation decisions.

payload.description: Enable children to view available and completed task inventory and claim completed tasks on a weekly basis
payload.justification: Signal SG049 expresses a fundamental enterprise goal of task claiming and tracking capability for child users. | Signal SG051 captures the strategic purpose of task inventory transparency for child users.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with monetary values
payload.justification: Signal SG050 articulates a strategic goal to provide parents task definition and compensation configuration capability.

payload.description: Enable children to view their weekly earnings summary for motivational purposes
payload.justification: Signal SG052 identifies a motivational goal centered on earnings visibility for child engagement.

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: Signal SG053 establishes a strategic goal of performance reporting supporting accurate payment processing.

payload.description: Provide stakeholders longitudinal task history access for accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: Signal SG054 expresses a strategic goal of historical data access supporting accountability and analytical capabilities.

payload.description: Parent specifies tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: SG291 describes the parent capability to specify task entities and assign monetary values as a business process.

payload.description: Child claims completed task for reward tracking
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the core business process of children claiming tasks, and SG292 provides the linking mechanism.

payload.description: Child views available and completed tasks within time scope
payload.justification: SG293 defines the child visibility capability as a business function for accessing both task states.

payload.description: System aggregates weekly earnings totals for child
payload.justification: SG294 establishes the weekly aggregation capability as a distinct business function producing earnings summaries.

payload.description: Parent views child-task-earnings report for payment fulfillment
payload.justification: SG295 defines the parent reporting capability as a business function supporting payment decision-making.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly boundaries
payload.justification: SG296 explicitly specifies a cyclical business rule governing when task availability is reset.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention and accessibility requirements as a business rule governing data lifecycle.

payload.description: Task with monetary value assigned by parent
payload.justification: SG291 explicitly defines task entities with associated monetary value attributes specified by parents.

payload.description: Child actor's completed task record
payload.justification: SG292 specifies completed task entities linked to child entities, representing distinct records of task completion.

payload.description: Weekly earnings aggregate for child
payload.justification: SG294 defines weekly aggregation of earned monetary totals as a distinct business data object visible to children.

payload.description: Historical task data retained across time periods
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention of historical task data entities, making them distinct business objects spanning temporal periods.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings association tracking completed tasks for payment
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the core business relationship of children claiming tasks, and SG292 specifies the linkage mechanism between child and completed task entities. | SG295 explicitly defines the three-way relationship between child, task, and earnings entities for payment fulfillment reporting.

payload.description: Weekly period for task availability and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: SG294 and SG296 both establish weekly temporal boundaries as the fundamental business cycle for task reset and earnings calculation.

payload.description: Task completion by child
payload.justification: SG290, SG292, and SG293 reference completed tasks as a distinct business event that triggers state changes and visibility updates.

payload.description: Parent who assigns tasks and reviews earnings
payload.justification: SG291 and SG295 identify parent as a distinct business actor with task specification and reporting responsibilities.

payload.description: Child who claims tasks and views earnings
payload.justification: SG290, SG293, and SG294 identify child as a distinct business actor with task claiming, viewing, and earnings tracking interactions.

payload.description: Track children's earned monetary rewards for completed tasks
payload.justification: SG290 establishes the core business concept of monetary reward tracking as the fundamental goal of the system.

payload.description: Support parent fulfillment of payment obligations
payload.justification: SG295 explicitly states that parent reporting capability exists to support payment fulfillment, indicating this as a business objective.

payload.description: Child visibility limited to temporal scope of tasks
payload.justification: SG293 defines visibility requirements within temporal scope as a business constraint on what children can access.

payload.description: Historical task data must remain accessible across periods
payload.justification: SG297 mandates retention and accessibility as a business constraint on data lifecycle management.

payload.description: Data visibility rule governing child user access to task collections
payload.justification: Signal specifies normative access pattern requirements defining logical constraints on data visibility, representing a design rule for access control.

payload.description: Parental aggregation and presentation of child's weekly earnings and task completion to child
payload.justification: Signal describes temporal aggregation logic as a functional capability for computing and displaying weekly earnings, representing a logical function. | Signal defines a logical reporting interface as a functional capability providing aggregated analytical views to parent role.

payload.description: Task completion event association to child entity
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a logical association rule linking task completion events to child entities, representing a conceptual relationship in the data model.

payload.description: Weekly task availability restoration cycle
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes periodic state reset logic defining weekly cyclical behavior, representing a temporal cycle event.

payload.description: Historical task record persistence and accessibility requirement
payload.justification: Signal mandates data retention as a normative requirement constraining system design, representing a logical constraint on data lifecycle.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions—a technical constraint governing deployment decisions at the Builder level.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle for earned amount totals access
payload.justification: Signal SG542 establishes a weekly operational schedule boundary, representing a detailed timing specification for when child actors can access earned totals.

payload.description: Weekly task availability reset cycle
payload.justification: Signal SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task availability resets, representing a detailed sequencing specification for system state refresh operations.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 explicitly identifies iOS as a distinct deployment platform. Per Rule 5, each named platform requires a separate CCI at the Implementer level, representing a specific physical deployment target.

payload.description: Android mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 explicitly identifies Android as a distinct deployment platform. Per Rule 5, each named platform requires a separate CCI at the Implementer level, representing a specific physical deployment target.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: Signal SG545 explicitly identifies Windows as a distinct deployment platform. Per Rule 5, each named platform requires a separate CCI at the Implementer level, representing a specific physical deployment target.

payload.description: Child user executing task claiming operations
payload.justification: Signal SG539 explicitly identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities. At the Implementer level, this specifies a distinct user interface actor with task claiming capabilities.

payload.description: Parent user performing task configuration and value assignment operations
payload.justification: Signal SG540 explicitly identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment. At the Implementer level, this specifies a distinct administrative user interface actor.

payload.description: Parent role with report access and pocket money distribution authority
payload.justification: Signal SG543 defines the parent operational role specifically for accessing reports and executing distribution activities, representing a detailed role specification with defined access permissions.

payload.description: Child actor visibility constraint requiring access to available and completed task views within weekly cycles
payload.justification: Signal SG541 defines operational visibility requirements as a constraint on what child actors can view, specifying detailed validation criteria for user interface access within temporal boundaries.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
